title: multidevice: PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE handler wires device_id
description:
Update the PUBLISH_KEY_PACKAGE handler in relay/src/ws.rs to extract device_id from the request params and pass it through to store.

Why: once the protocol and store both support device_id, the relay handler is the glue. This is the smallest of the ws.rs changes.

What to do:
- Extract device_id from PublishKeyPackageParams (added in nie-3s3r)
- Call store.upsert_key_package(pub_id, device_id, data) instead of the current single-arg form
- On disconnect, call store.delete_key_packages_for_device(pub_id, device_id) to clean up stale packages
- KEY_PACKAGE_READY broadcast stays the same (pub_id is sufficient for peers to know a new package is available)

Acceptance: relay stores per-device KeyPackages; reconnecting device replaces only its own package, not other devices'; existing KEY_PACKAGE_READY behavior unchanged.
close_reason: connection_device_id local var tracks published device; deleted via delete_device_key_package on disconnect; ws.rs already passes device_id to save_key_package from nie-1zcu
